breakdown: Human, belonging to humankind; derived from manu, the archetypal human.

mārisa.meaning: sir; dear sir (respectful form of address)

māsaddhamāsā.meaning: a month and a half-month; months and fortnights
māsaddhamāsā.summary: This is a compound meaning "month + half-month", i.e. periods of a month and a fortnight.
breakdown: Half-month, fortnight (addha "half" + māsa "month"; the doubled dd reflects compound sandhi).

mātikāpariyosānā.meaning: ending with the summaries; concluding with the mātikā (the schematic lists of the Abhidhamma)
mātikāpariyosānā.summary: This is a compound meaning "summary list + termination".
breakdown: Order, list, summary; the introductory summary or schematic concept-list that opens texts of the Abhidhamma.
mātikāpariyosānā.goenka: In the tradition's study material this term is explained with reference to the Abhidhamma: the mātikā is the introductory summary, the presented concept-list of an Abhidhamma text, and mātikāpariyosānā means reaching the termination of those lists. Klaus Nothnagel refers readers to lesson 3.1.13, Abhidhamma — Introduction and Terminology, for fuller detail.

mātugāmena.meaning: woman; womenfolk (instrumental: with or by a woman)
mātugāmena.gram: masculine noun, compound; instrumental singular of mātugāma
breakdown: Literally "village, collection"; mātugāma is the standard idiom for womankind, women collectively.

mātā.gram: feminine noun, nominative singular of mātar
breakdown: Mother; the -ar stem noun, nominative singular mātā.

māṇava.meaning: young man; young brahmin; brahmin student
breakdown: The archetypal human; māṇava is literally "descendant of manu", hence a youth, especially a young brahmin.

mūgabbataṁ.meaning: the vow of dumbness; the ascetic practice of keeping mute
mūgabbataṁ.summary: This is a compound meaning "dumb + practice", an ascetic observance of total silence.
breakdown: Vow, religious practice, observance. (The bb arises by compound sandhi of -a + va-.)

mūlaghaccaṁ.meaning: destroyed at the root; cut off root and all
breakdown: To be destroyed, destruction (from √han "to strike, kill").

mūḷhaṁ.meaning: gone astray; having lost one's way; deluded
mūḷhaṁ.gram: past participle of muyhati (√muh), accusative/neuter singular
breakdown: To be confused, deluded; the past participle √muh + ta gives mūḍha, in Pāli mūḷha.

mūḷho.gram: past participle of muyhati (√muh), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: To be confused, deluded; the past participle √muh + ta gives mūḍha, in Pāli mūḷha.
To dance; nacca is the action noun "dancing".

nadīviduggaṁ.meaning: a river difficult to cross; a treacherous river-ford
nadīviduggaṁ.summary: This is a compound meaning "river + very hard to pass".
breakdown: Difficult terrain, hard to pass (du "bad, hard" + gga from √gam "go").

nakkosakaparibhāsako.meaning: not one who reviles and abuses
nakkosakaparibhāsako.summary: This is a negated compound: "not (someone who is) reviling + abusing".
nakkosakaparibhāsako.gram: masculine adjective, negated compound, nominative singular
breakdown: Negative prefix "not" (here fused as na- with doubling of the following k).
One who reviles, an abuser with words.

namassāma.meaning: we pay respects; we revere, we bow down
namassāma.gram: verb, 1st person plural present of namassati (√namass)
breakdown: To pay homage, revere (denominative from namo "homage").

nandīrāgasahagatā.meaning: bound up with pleasure and lust; accompanied by delight and sensuous craving
nandīrāgasahagatā.summary: This is a compound describing craving (taṇhā): "enjoyment + lust + going together with".
breakdown: Going together with, accompanied by (saha "together" + gata "gone").
nandīrāgasahagatā.goenka: The term appears in the Dhammacakkappavattana and Mahāsatipaṭṭhāna expositions of the Second Noble Truth: the craving that leads to repeated becoming is nandīrāgasahagatā, bound up with pleasure and lust, finding delight now here, now there. It characterizes how taṇhā arises and gets established wherever in the world of mind and matter there is something enticing and pleasurable.

nappajappanti.meaning: they do not crave; they do not prattle or long for
nappajappanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present, negated (na + pajappati, √japp)
breakdown: Not. (The doubled pp is sandhi doubling after na.)
To prattle; to crave, long for (pa + √japp).

nappasaṁsanti.meaning: they do not praise; they do not commend
nappasaṁsanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present, negated (na + pasaṁsati, √saṁs)
breakdown: Not. (The doubled pp is sandhi doubling after na.)

narāsabhaṁ.meaning: bull among men; chief of men (an epithet of the Buddha)
narāsabhaṁ.summary: This is a compound meaning "man + bull", i.e. the noblest, most eminent of men.
breakdown: Bull; figuratively a leader, the most eminent one. (The long ā arises by vowel coalescence at the compound boundary.)
To bend, incline; nati is the abstract noun "bending, inclination".
There is (3rd person singular of √as "to be").

natthika.meaning: one who denies existence; holding the view "there is not" (an annihilationist)
breakdown: Adjectival suffix: characterized by the view that "there is not".

nayahetu.meaning: by inference; by reason of logical method
breakdown: Cause, reason; in compounds "on account of".

nayidaṁ.note: uncertain: the lexicon's glossary row pairs this headword with the gloss "not + clinging to existence" and breakdown an + upādāya, which belongs to anupādāya; the row appears misaligned. The meaning given here follows the form itself (na + idaṁ).

naḷova.meaning: like a reed; just as a reed
naḷova.gram: sandhi fusion: masculine noun + emphatic particle (naḷa + iva/eva)
breakdown: Like; just as (fused with the preceding vowel to -ova).

ne.gram: pronoun, masculine accusative plural of ta (alternative form)

negama.meaning: belonging to a town; townsfolk, town-dweller
breakdown: Market town (ni + √gam); the derivative negama, with strengthened first vowel, means "belonging to a town".

negamajānapadesu.summary: This is a compound meaning "townfolk + countryfolk", here in the locative plural.
breakdown: Townsfolk; belonging to a town (from nigama "market town").
Countryfolk; people of the countryside (from janapada "country, district").

nekapariyāyena.meaning: in many ways; by various methods
breakdown: Many, various (literally "not one"; here with initial a- elided to neka- by sandhi).

nekatikā.gram: masculine noun/adjective (from nikati "fraud"), plural or feminine form
breakdown: Suffix forming an agent or characterizing adjective: one given to fraud.

nekkhammasaṅkappo.meaning: thought of renunciation; intention of renunciation
nekkhammasaṅkappo.summary: This is a compound meaning "renunciation + thought", one of the three right thoughts of sammāsaṅkappa.
breakdown: Renunciation; giving up of sensual craving and worldly life.
nekkhammasaṅkappo.goenka: In the suttas studied in the tradition, nekkhammasaṅkappa is one of the three kinds of right thought (sammāsaṅkappa) of the Noble Eightfold Path, alongside thoughts free from ill will and from cruelty: thoughts of renunciation, turned away from craving for sensual pleasures.

breakdown: Not (contracted with the following vowel to ne-).
Fault, sin; hence nela "faultless". Also appears as anela with the a- form of the negative.

nemittikatā.meaning: hinting; insinuation (an improper way of seeking gains)
breakdown: Adjectival suffix: one who gives signs or hints.
Abstract noun suffix: the practice of hinting.

nesaṁ.gram: pronoun, genitive/dative plural of ta (alternative form)

nesohamasmi.meaning: I am not this; this I am not
nesohamasmi.summary: This is a sandhi fusion of four words, part of the formula "netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā" — "this is not mine, this I am not, this is not my self".
nesohamasmi.gram: sandhi fusion: indeclinable + pronoun + pronoun + verb
nesohamasmi.goenka: This phrase belongs to the canonical formula of insight into anattā: of each aggregate the meditator understands, in line with reality, "this is not mine, this I am not, this is not my self". In the tradition this is not a philosophical assertion to be believed but an understanding to be verified directly through the experience of the arising and passing away of mind and matter.

nesā.gram: sandhi fusion: indeclinable + pronoun (na + esā, feminine)

nesādakule.meaning: in a family of hunters; in a hunter clan

nete.gram: sandhi fusion: indeclinable + pronoun (na + ete, masculine plural)

nevajjhagā.meaning: did not thus attain; not thus obtained
nevajjhagā.gram: sandhi fusion: indeclinable + indeclinable + aorist verb
breakdown: Attained, obtained (aorist of adhigacchati). (The doubled jj is sandhi doubling.)

nibbattate.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, middle voice of nibbattati (ni + √vatt)
breakdown: Prefix: out, forth. (The doubled bb arises by assimilation of nir + v.)
To turn, proceed; nibbattati means to arise, come into being.

nibbattati.meaning: springs up; is born, is produced, grows, arises
breakdown: Prefix: out, forth. (The doubled bb arises by assimilation of nir + v.)
To turn, proceed; nibbattati means to spring up, be produced, arise.

nibbattissati.meaning: will be reborn; will spring up, will arise
nibbattissati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular future of nibbattati (ni + √vatt)
breakdown: To spring up, be born, arise (ni + √vatt).
Prefix: out, through. (The doubled bb arises by assimilation of nir + v.)
To pierce; nibbedha means penetration, piercing through.

nibbedhikāya.meaning: piercing, penetrating, discriminating (dative/genitive: of or for penetrating insight)
nibbedhikāya.gram: adjective, feminine dative/genitive singular of nibbedhika
